name: New York, New York
seo_title: New York, New York Sales Tax Calculator
meta_description: The combined sales tax rate in New York, New York is 8.875%. Enter a price to calculate the exact sales tax and total by product category.
intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in New York, New York is 8.875%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in New York, New York?

The sales tax rate in New York is 8.875%. That combines New York's statewide rate of 4% with 4.875% in local tax collected by New York County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the New York sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 8.875%. A $100.00 purchase in New York adds about $8.88, for a total of $108.88. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to New York County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how New York treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the New York rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. If you sell here, it's worth confirming the current figure before each filing — Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction and files automatically.

intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in Oakland, California is 10.75%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in Oakland, California?

The sales tax rate in Oakland is 10.75%. That combines California's statewide rate of 6.25% with 4.5% in local tax collected by Alameda County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Oakland sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 10.75%. A $100.00 purchase in Oakland adds about $10.75, for a total of $110.75. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Alameda County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how California treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Oakland rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. If you sell here, it's worth confirming the current figure before each filing — Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction and files automatically.

meta_description: The combined sales tax rate in Garden Grove, California is 8.75%. Enter a price to calculate the exact sales tax and total by product category.
intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in Garden Grove, California is 8.75%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in Garden Grove, California?

The sales tax rate in Garden Grove is 8.75%. That combines California's statewide rate of 6.25% with 2.5% in local tax collected by Orange County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Garden Grove sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 8.75%. A $100.00 purchase in Garden Grove adds about $8.75, for a total of $108.75. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Orange County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how California treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Garden Grove rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. If you sell here, it's worth confirming the current figure before each filing — Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction and files automatically.

meta_description: The combined sales tax rate in Simi Valley, California is 7.25%. Enter a price to calculate the exact sales tax and total by product category.
intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in Simi Valley, California is 7.25%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in Simi Valley, California?

The sales tax rate in Simi Valley is 7.25%. That combines California's statewide rate of 6.25% with 1% in local tax collected by Ventura County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Simi Valley sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 7.25%. A $100.00 purchase in Simi Valley adds about $7.25, for a total of $107.25. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Ventura County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how California treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Simi Valley rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. If you sell here, it's worth confirming the current figure before each filing — Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction and files automatically.

How much is sales tax in Wilmington, North Carolina?

The sales tax rate in Wilmington is 7%. That combines North Carolina's statewide rate of 4.75% with 2.25% in local tax collected by New Hanover County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Wilmington sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 7%. A $100.00 purchase in Wilmington adds about $7.00, for a total of $107.00. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to New Hanover County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how North Carolina treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Wilmington rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. meta_description: The combined sales tax rate in Green Bay, Wisconsin is 5.5%. Enter a price to calculate the exact sales tax and total by product category.
intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in Green Bay, Wisconsin is 5.5%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in Green Bay, Wisconsin?

The sales tax rate in Green Bay is 5.5%. That combines Wisconsin's statewide rate of 5% with 0.5% in local tax collected by Brown County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Green Bay sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 5.5%. A $100.00 purchase in Green Bay adds about $5.50, for a total of $105.50. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Brown County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how Wisconsin treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Green Bay rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. If you sell here, it's worth confirming the current figure before each filing — Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction and files automatically.

intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in Meridian, Idaho is 6%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in Meridian, Idaho?

The sales tax rate in Meridian is 6%. That combines Idaho's statewide rate of 6% with 0% in local tax collected by Ada County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Meridian sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 6%. A $100.00 purchase in Meridian adds about $6.00, for a total of $106.00. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Ada County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how Idaho treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Meridian rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter. If you sell here, it's worth confirming the current figure before each filing — Zamp tracks those changes across every U.S. jurisdiction and files automatically.

intro_text: The combined sales tax rate in Pasco, Washington is 8.9%, made up of state, county, and local district rates. Enter a purchase amount below to see the exact tax for what you're buying.
seo_content:
How much is sales tax in Pasco, Washington?

The sales tax rate in Pasco is 8.9%. That combines Washington's statewide rate of 6.5% with 2.4% in local tax collected by Franklin County and any city or special districts covering the address. Local rates aren't the same across a metro area, so the total can change from one ZIP code to the next — the Pasco sales tax calculator above uses the local rate so your estimate matches what you'd actually pay at checkout.

Working out the tax on a purchase

To do it by hand, multiply the price by 8.9%. A $100.00 purchase in Pasco adds about $8.90, for a total of $108.90. The calculator handles this for any amount and breaks the result down by jurisdiction, so you can see how much goes to the state, to Franklin County, and to local districts. Enter a price and choose a category to get an exact figure for your purchase.

What's taxable, and what isn't

Not everything is taxed the same way. Most tangible goods are fully taxable, but groceries and prescription medicine are often exempt or taxed at a reduced rate, and some services fall outside sales tax altogether. Prepared food, clothing, and general merchandise are usually taxed in full. Switch the category in the calculator to see how Washington treats a specific purchase before you buy it or set up tax on what you sell.

Common questions

Why is the rate different in nearby cities? Counties, cities, and special districts each set their own local sales tax, and they don't all charge the same amount. A neighboring town can sit in a different district and land a fraction of a percent higher or lower, even when the state rate is identical.

How often does the Pasco rate change? State rates rarely move, but local rates can change when voters approve new district taxes, usually at the start of a calendar quarter.
